title: LiteParse
description: Extract text and metadata from PDFs, Office documents, and images using LiteParse (by LlamaIndex). Performs spatial PDF parsing via PDFium and optional OCR via Tesseract.

LITEPARSE_TESSERACT_BINARY.description: Path to the tesseract binary used to discover the system tessdata directory. Auto-installed by the plugin via brew/apt; not invoked at runtime — lit's bundled tesseract-rs does the actual OCR.

LITEPARSE_IMAGEMAGICK_BINARY.description: Path to an ImageMagick binary used by LiteParse for image conversion. Auto-installed by the plugin via brew/apt; not invoked directly by this hook.

LITEPARSE_TIMEOUT.description: Timeout for LiteParse extraction in seconds

LITEPARSE_FORMATS.description: Output formats to produce, one file per source. `text` writes `<input>.txt` (lit's plain layout-preserving text — what search backends index). `json` is also supported and writes `<input>.json` with lit v2's structured per-page output (text, bounding boxes, font metadata, OCR confidence); not indexed by search backends. Any subset of [text, json] is valid.

LITEPARSE_OCR_ENABLED.description: Run Tesseract OCR on pages/images without embedded text (passes --no-ocr when disabled)

LITEPARSE_OCR_LANGUAGE.description: Tesseract OCR language code (e.g. eng, fra, deu, chi_sim)

LITEPARSE_OCR_SERVER_URL.description: Optional HTTP OCR server URL (e.g. EasyOCR/PaddleOCR wrapper) to use instead of bundled Tesseract

LITEPARSE_TESSDATA_DIR.description: Path to a Tesseract tessdata directory containing <lang>.traineddata files. Auto-detected from common system locations when empty.

LITEPARSE_MAX_PAGES.description: Maximum pages to parse per document

LITEPARSE_TARGET_PAGES.description: Page range to parse, e.g. '1-5,10,15-20'. Empty means all pages.

LITEPARSE_DPI.description: Rendering DPI used when rasterising pages for OCR / images

LITEPARSE_MIN_IMAGE_DIMENSION.description: Skip image files (PNG/JPG/etc.) smaller than this many pixels in both width AND height. Avoids wasting OCR time on favicons, sprite thumbnails, and tracking pixels. Set to 0 to disable filtering.

LITEPARSE_BATCH_SIZE.description: Number of source documents processed per `lit batch-parse` invocation. Sources are sorted largest-first so the most content-rich files come back first. Smaller batches give finer-grained progressive output but slightly more lit-startup overhead.

LITEPARSE_MAX_SOURCES.description: Hard cap on number of documents to process per snapshot, applied after size-desc sort so the largest files always make it. Set to 0 for no cap.

LITEPARSE_PARALLEL_WORKERS.description: Number of concurrent `lit batch-parse` processes. Each process is pinned to --num-workers=1 internally (its own multi-worker mode hurts on Apple Silicon); outer parallelism via this knob gives near-linear speedup up to a few procs. Empirically 2 is enough to fit a typical content-heavy article under the 60s hook timeout.

LITEPARSE_ARGS_EXTRA.description: Extra arguments to append to LiteParse command
